evidence.quote: The reaction of dimethyl disulfide 1 with 2-endo-isocyanatobicyclo[2.2.1]heptane is an intermolecular reaction... TLC analysis showed a long streak without distinct spots... methyl peak bonded to the sulfur was not found in the 1H NMR spectrum

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: gave the expected thiocarbamate 4-S(CO)N in 65% yield
we started with the corresponding carboxylic acids and then converted them into the isocyanates by a Curtius rearrangement

issues.reason: Text states synthesis started from carboxylic acids converted via Curtius rearrangement (using DPPA). Listing the isocyanate as the input substrate contradicts the reagent (DPPA) function, though it matches the compound name '4' in the text.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Change substrate to the corresponding carboxylic acid precursor (e.g., 6-endo-(methyldisulfanyl)bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane-2-carboxylic acid) to align with the Curtius reagents.

evidence.quote: The structure of thiocarbamate 4-S(CO)N was unequivocally established by an X-ray crystallographic structure study, which is shown in Fig. 2
NBO analyses were carried out at the M06-2X/6-311+G(d,p) level.13... NBO, version 3.1... 14... Gaussian 09... 13
For isocyanate 3 produced by the Curtius rearrangement in toluene... interaction of 1.96 kcal mol-1... 3-Water... significant interaction of 5.18 kcal mol-1
4-toluene did not show any interaction... oxygen-protonated isocyanate 4-NCOH+-water... 3.22 kcal mol-1... and 1.69 kcal mol-1
